prompt: The raw type usage causes an unchecked warning and potential ClassCastException. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayList cannot mix String and int values
Using a raw List bypasses compile-time type checking; list.add(42) adds an Integer, and casting it to String at runtime throws ClassCastException
list.get() always returns Object and cannot be cast
Raw types are forbidden in Java 17 and cause compile errors
explanation: Raw types (List without type parameter) disable generics compile-time checking — you can add any object. list.get(1) returns the Integer 42. Casting Integer to String throws ClassCastException. Fix: use List<String> list = new ArrayList<>() so the compiler rejects list.add(42).
